questions.question: Graph the solution to $x + 2 \leq 7$ on a number line.

The ancient Egyptians built the Great Pyramid with a height of $146.5$ meters. If the height $h$ of the pyramid is reduced by $x$ meters, the inequality $h - x > 120$ represents the condition that the reduced height is greater than $120$ meters. Solve for $x$.
The Babylonians used a sexagesimal (base-60) system for mathematics. If $x$ is a number in this system and $x + 20 > 40$, solve for $x$.
The ancient Greeks used the method of exhaustion to calculate areas. If $x$ is the area of a shape and $x - 3 \geq 5$, solve for $x$.
Write the solution to $-3x + 2 > 5$ in interval notation.
The ancient Chinese used abacuses for calculations. If $x$ is a number on an abacus and $-2x + 1 > 7$, solve for $x$.
Solve the inequality $x^2 - 4x - 3 > 0$ and graph the solution on a number line. Write your answer in interval notation.

The ancient Romans built a bridge with an arch that had a height of $15$ meters. If the height $h$ of the bridge is reduced by $x$ meters, the inequality $h - x > 10$ represents the condition that the reduced height is greater than $10$ meters. Solve for $x$, graph the solution on a number line, and write your answer in interval notation.

teacher_rules: Tip 1: Emphasize the importance of understanding the inequality sign and its direction.
Tip 2: Use number lines to visualize the solution to inequalities.
Tip 3: Encourage students to check their solutions by plugging values back into the original inequality.
